title: Verify free trial end date display and no charges before trial ends, cancellation stops billing, subscription management accessible
preconditions: User has an account and is on a free trial. App is installed and user is logged in. Payment method is linked.
steps: 1. Open the app and go to Account settings.
2. Check that the free trial end date is clearly displayed.
3. Verify that no charge has been applied before the trial end date (check payment history or receipt).
4. Cancel the subscription during the trial period from the in-app subscription management.
5. Confirm cancellation confirmation message appears.
6. Wait until after the trial end date and verify no billing occurs.
7. Verify that the subscription management option is accessible under Account settings.
expected_result: User sees the trial end date. No charges before trial ends. Cancellation immediately stops future billing. Subscription management is available in-app under Account settings.

title: Verify paid users can log in immediately after purchase without 'account does not exist' errors and session persists
preconditions: User completes a purchase (subscription) successfully. App is installed and user is logged out or app restarted.
steps: 1. After purchase, close and reopen the app.
2. Attempt to log in with the same credentials used during purchase.
3. Observe the login result and any error messages.
4. Complete a workout or navigate through the app.
5. Force close the app and reopen it.
6. Verify the user is still logged in and session persists.
expected_result: User logs in successfully without 'account does not exist' error. User session persists across app restarts.

title: Verify free tier ad length is max 30 seconds, skippable after 5 seconds, close button visible, no external redirects, does not interrupt workout flow
preconditions: User is on free tier. App is installed, user is logged in, and a workout is started.
steps: 1. Start a workout session (any free workout).
2. When an ad plays, note the start time.
3. Observe the ad duration. It should not exceed 30 seconds.
4. Check if a 'Skip' or close button appears after 5 seconds (or earlier).
5. Tap the close button/skip and verify the ad closes and workout resumes.
6. During the ad, check that there are no automatic redirects to external websites or app store pages.
7. Complete the workout and verify the ad did not interrupt the flow (e.g., no forced pauses after ad).
expected_result: Ad length <= 30 seconds. Skippable after 5 seconds. Close button visible and tappable. No external website redirects. Ads do not interrupt workout flow.

title: Verify at least 5 free workouts per category, clear 'Free' vs 'Premium' labels, and previously saved free workouts are restored
preconditions: User has an account. App is installed and logged in. Some free workouts were previously saved before the update.
steps: 1. Open the app and navigate to the workout library.
2. Check categories: Strength, Cardio, Yoga.
3. Count the number of free workouts (not requiring subscription) available in each category. Verify at least 5 per category.
4. For a workout card, observe if there is a clear label indicating 'Free' or 'Premium'.
5. Go to the user's saved workouts list. Check if previously saved free workouts (that were locked) are now accessible again.
6. Attempt to start one of those restored workouts and complete it.
expected_result: At least 5 free workouts per category. Workout cards have clear 'Free'/'Premium' labels. Previously saved free workouts are restored and playable.

title: Verify new workouts are added weekly, no consecutive identical workouts in daily recommendations, and user feedback option exists
preconditions: App is installed and user is logged in. It is a new week since last update.
steps: 1. Open the app and go to the daily recommendation section.
2. Review the list of recommended workouts for today. Check that no two consecutive workouts are identical (same exercise set).
3. Note the date and the set of workouts shown.
4. After one week (or simulate a week via time change if possible), return to the daily recommendations.
5. Verify that at least one new workout appears that was not present the previous week.
6. Navigate to a workout's detail page and look for a feedback option (e.g., a thumbs up/down or comment button).
7. Submit feedback and confirm it is accepted.
expected_result: New workouts appear weekly. No consecutive identical workouts in daily recommendations. User feedback option is available and functional.

title: Verify Apple Watch integration for automatic calorie tracking: connection, calories in summary, reliable data sync
preconditions: User has an Apple Watch paired with iPhone. App is installed and user is logged in.
steps: 1. Open the app and go to Settings > Integrations.
2. Look for 'Apple Watch' option and tap to connect.
3. Follow the prompts to authorize health data access.
4. Start a workout on the iPhone app (or Apple Watch companion if available).
5. Complete the workout and view the workout summary.
6. Verify that calories burned are displayed in the summary and match Apple Health data (approximate).
7. Disconnect from Apple Watch, reconnect, and check that past workout data is still available.
expected_result: User can connect Apple Watch in Settings > Integrations. Calories burned appear in workout summary. Data syncs reliably (no data loss after disconnect/reconnect).

title: Verify rep counting toggle per exercise, tap button to count each rep, total reps recorded in workout log
preconditions: App is installed and user is logged in. User is on a workout that supports rep counting (e.g., strength training).
steps: 1. Start a workout session and select an exercise that has a timer by default.
2. During exercise setup, check for a toggle that allows switching from timer to manual rep counting.
3. Toggle to manual rep counting mode.
4. As you perform the exercise, tap the on-screen button each time you complete a rep.
5. Complete the set and move to the next exercise.
6. After the workout, go to the workout log/history.
7. Verify that the total reps for that exercise are recorded correctly.
expected_result: Toggle appears per exercise during setup. User can tap a button to count each rep. Total reps recorded accurately in workout log.

title: Verify pregnancy-safe workouts, new difficulty levels, abdominal exercises, and subtle sound indicators
preconditions: App is installed and user is logged in. Content version includes v2.0 updates.
steps: 1. Open the app and navigate to the workout library.
2. Search for 'pregnancy' or filter by special populations. Check that at least 5 pregnancy-safe workouts exist.
3. In filter/settings for workout difficulty, verify there are 5 new difficulty levels beyond the standard beginner/intermediate/advanced (e.g., 'Beginner 1-5' or 'Difficulty 1-5').
4. Go to the abdominal exercises category and count distinct exercises. Verify at least 10 are available.
5. Start a pregnancy-safe workout that includes audio instructions.
6. In the settings or during workout, find an option for sound indicators (e.g., 'Sound cues' or 'Beeps').
7. Enable 'Subtle sound indicators' and start the workout.
8. Verify that instead of continuous music, short beeps or subtle cues are played for exercise transitions.
expected_result: At least 5 pregnancy-safe workouts available. 5 new difficulty levels present. 10 new abdominal exercises. Option for subtle sound cues (short beeps) available and functional.
